breach.summary: Most of this week's trouble came dressed as something useful. A package stole data. A fake extension opened remote access. A safety app became spyware. An image gave hidden orders to an AI agent. Other threats hid in open systems, weak code, and normal network traffic. The threats change every week. Subscribe, and...
breach.org: ThreatsDay: Android Spyware, PLC Attacks, AI Image Prompt Injection + 12 More Stories

breach.summary: Hackers recently obtained non-sensitive customer information and other documents from the company. The post Upbound Group Says Data Breach Led to $13 Million in Fraudulent Contract Losses appeared first on SecurityWeek.
breach.org: Upbound Group Says Data Breach Led to $13 Million in Fraudulent Contract Losses

breach.summary: The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]
breach.org: Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases

breach.summary: South Korea disclosed that hackers breached the National Diplomatic Academy's online education system for ten months and stole personal information belonging to current and former employees of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA), including overseas diplomats. [...]
breach.org: South Korea discloses data breach impacting diplomats worldwide

breach.summary: Hackers leaked names, email addresses, phone numbers, passwords, and financial information stolen from the two platforms. The post Suno, Paidwork Data Breaches Affect Tens of Millions of Accounts appeared first on SecurityWeek.
breach.org: Suno, Paidwork Data Breaches Affect Tens of Millions of Accounts

breach.summary: An attacker only needed to convince the targeted user to visit a malicious website to exfiltrate WhatsApp messages and contacts. The post Flaw in Adobe Extension With 300M Installs Enabled WhatsApp Data Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.
breach.org: Flaw in Adobe Extension With 300M Installs Enabled WhatsApp Data Theft

cve.summary: A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client. The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organization's entire email directory, the password saved in the browser and the codes kept for two-factor recovery. Opening...
cve.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes

cve.summary: A nine-year-old race condition vulnerability in the Linux kernel's XFS filesystem, tracked as CVE-2026-64600, allows local attackers to overwrite protected files and gain root privileges. [...]
cve.title: New RefluXFS Linux flaw lets attackers gain root privileges

cve.summary: Israeli cybersecurity firm Check Point Software has addressed an actively exploited zero-day flaw in the company's SmartConsole graphical user interface (GUI) admin panel. [...]
cve.title: Check Point warns of SmartConsole zero-day exploited in attacks

cve.summary: RefluXFS, a new Linux kernel flaw disclosed on July 22 and tracked as CVE-2026-64600, lets an unprivileged local user overwrite root-owned files on an XFS filesystem and gain persistent root access. Qualys said default installations of Red Hat Enterprise Linux and its derivatives, Fedora Server, and Amazon Linux...
cve.title: Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs

cve.summary: The vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-16232 has been exploited against customers with certain configurations. The post New Check Point Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.
cve.title: New Check Point Zero-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild

cve.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in snap-confine that an unprivileged user can trigger to obtain root access and gain complete control of a target environment. The high-severity flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8933 (CVSS score: 7.8), impacts default...
cve.title: Ubuntu snap-confine Flaw Could Give Local Users Root on Default Desktop Installs

cve.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched vulnerability chain in the Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension that has over 314 million users, which, if exploited, could facilitate a silent hijack of a user's WhatsApp data. The shortcoming has been codenamed HermeticReader by Guardio Labs. It's...
cve.title: Adobe Acrobat Extension Flaw Let Malicious Sites Read WhatsApp Web Data

cve.summary: A high-severity security flaw impacting open-source developer platform Windmill has come under active exploitation in the wild, per VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29059 (CVSS score: 7.5), a case of unauthenticated path traversal impacting Windmill's "get_log_file" endpoint...
cve.title: Hackers Exploit Windmill Flaw to Read Arbitrary Server Files Without Authentication

threat.summary: CISA is warning that the Russian state-sponsored hacking group Laundry Bear, also known as Void Blizzard, is targeting organizations using Zimbra Collaboration email servers by combining phishing attacks with the exploitation of a now-patched Zimbra vulnerability. [...]
threat.actor: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra zero-click flaw for email theft

threat.summary: Ukraine's CERT has uncovered attacks distributing an archive containing the legitimate Notepad++ application and a malicious utility called LunchPoke disguised as a plugin to establish persistence. [...]
threat.actor: Hackers abuse Notepad++ plugins to stealthily install malware

threat.summary: Threat actors used credentials obtained from other companies to hack into Chick-fil-A One accounts. The post Chick-fil-A Accounts Get Fried in Credential Stuffing Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
threat.actor: Chick-fil-A Accounts Get Fried in Credential Stuffing Attack

threat.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a large-scale campaign that has turned compromised GitHub repositories into distributed attack infrastructure designed to target cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) instances. The activity involves malicious Packagist development versions spanning 10 packages associated...
threat.actor: Attackers Weaponize GitHub Actions Runners to Target cPanel and WHM Servers

threat.summary: SentinelOne’s new benchmark, built on the Fast16 case, shows which AI models can sustain a malware investigation and which cannot. The post Nuclear-Sabotage Malware Benchmark Trips Up Most Frontier AI Models appeared first on SecurityWeek.
threat.actor: Nuclear-Sabotage Malware Benchmark Trips Up Most Frontier AI Models

threat.summary: Portuguese businesses operate in the same native language as Brazilian hackers, making those businesses easy targets.
threat.actor: Brazilian Banking Trojan Actively Spreading in Portugal

threat.summary: Sandworm_Mode is an early example of malware that exploits trusted AI tools and workflows to make malicious activity virtually indistinguishable from normal activity.
threat.actor: Attackers Are Learning to Live Off the AI Toolchain

threat.summary: A malicious application delivers four-stage Android spyware via phony Google Play sites, exploiting civilian fear during Iranian missile strikes.
threat.actor: Fake Bahrain Alert App Deploys Android Surveillance Malware

news.summary: Microsoft is impacted by a massive outage affecting Teams and Microsoft 365 services, primarily affecting users in North America. [...]
news.title: Microsoft 365 outage affects Teams, SharePoint and other services

news.summary: FedRAMP 20X replaces point-in-time assessments with continuous, machine-readable evidence that demonstrates security controls are working. Anecdotes explains what the transition from Rev5 to FedRAMP 20X means and how organizations can prepare for continuous, evidence-based assurance. [...]
news.title: FedRAMP Rev5 Is Ending: What the 20x Transition Really Requires

news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a sandbox escape vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Cowork that makes it possible to break out of the confines of a Linux virtual machine (VM) within which the agent runs to read or write files anywhere on the Mac. Accomplish AI, which shared details of the vulnerability...
news.title: Claude Cowork Flaw Could Let AI Agent Escape Its VM and Access Mac Files

news.summary: An exposed Alibaba Cloud server has revealed a China-nexus operation that Group-IB tracks as JadeProx. The cluster has targeted government, healthcare, and education organizations across Asia and Latin America with a previously undocumented Windows loader called TriBack Loader. Group-IB found the server in...
news.title: China-Nexus JadeProx Uses New TriBack Loader in Government and Healthcare Attacks

news.summary: Beginning July 27, 2026, GitHub will cut public bug bounty payouts by at least half at every severity level. Critical findings will drop from $20,000-$30,000+ to a fixed $10,000, while its permanent invite-only VIP tier will pay $30,000 or more. Reports filed before that date, including those already in GitHub's...
news.title: GitHub Cuts Public Bug Bounty Payouts, Moves Top Rewards to VIP Tier

news.summary: German and US law enforcement have taken down the core infrastructure of Kratos, described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing kits, and Indonesian authorities arrested the man they say developed and ran it. In a joint announcement on Monday, the Frankfurt public...
news.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA

news.summary: Google's DeepMind on Tuesday announced the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, a specialized artificial intelligence (AI) model built atop 3.5 Flash that's designed to discover, validate, and patch vulnerabilities quickly and efficiently. According to the tech giant, the model will be exclusively available to...
news.title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber AI to Find and Fix Software Vulnerabilities

news.summary: AgentForger allows an attacker to create, insert and remotely control an invisible autonomous AI agent inside a victim organization. The post OpenAI Fixes ChatGPT Agent Flaw That Could Let Attackers Forge an AI Insider appeared first on SecurityWeek.
news.title: OpenAI Fixes ChatGPT Agent Flaw That Could Let Attackers Forge an AI Insider

news.summary: The European Commission fined Google €890 million ($1 billion) on Thursday after finding the company had violated the European Union's Digital Markets Act (DMA), which ensures fair online competition. [...]
news.title: EU fines Google $1 billion for search, app store antitrust violations

news.summary: Microsoft is working to resolve an ongoing Exchange Online issue that has been mistakenly quarantining customers' mailboxes since Sunday. [...]
news.title: Microsoft working to fix Exchange Online mailbox quarantine issue

news.summary: Eclypsium has launched InfraTrust, a new infrastructure cybersecurity knowledge base and monthly InfraTrust Pulse report designed to help organizations prioritize vulnerabilities affecting infrastructure, firmware, networking, and edge devices. [...]
news.title: New InfraTrust report reveals infrastructure flaws admins should patch first

news.summary: The Adobe Acrobat extension for Chrome could be used to access conversations and data rendered in WhatsApp Web without any form of authentication. [...]
news.title: Adobe Chrome extension flaw let sites access private WhatsApp chats

kev.action: Apply mitigations in accordance with vendor instructions, ensuring compliance with CISA’s BOD 26-04 Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk (see URL in Notes) guidance and CISA’s “Forensics Triage Requirements” (see URL in Notes). Follow applicable BOD 26-04 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable. Stakeholders are responsible for evaluating each asset's internet exposure and ensuring adherence to BOD 26-04 patching guidelines.

patch.summary: Check Point has released security updates to address multiple vulnerabilities impacting Security Management and Multi-Domain Management (MDSM) products, including a critical flaw that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The security flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-16232 (CVSS score: 9.3), is an authentication...
patch.title: Check Point Patches Exploited SmartConsole Flaw Allowing Full Admin Access

ransomware.summary: The Chaos ransomware group ran its command-and-control through the victim's own browser. Cisco Talos on Thursday detailed msaRAT, the Rust implant behind it, found on a compromised Windows machine ahead of the encryptor. The implant never opens an outbound connection of its own. Its process talks to 127.0.0.1 and...
ransomware.group: Chaos Ransomware Uses msaRAT to Route C2 Traffic Through Headless Chrome and Edge

ransomware.summary: The Chaos ransomware gang is using a new backdoor dubbed msaRAT that hides command-and-control (C2) communication by routing it through the Chrome or Edge browsers. [...]
ransomware.group: New msaRAT malware uses Chrome, Edge browsers to route C2 traffic

ransomware.summary: A cyberattack on a food and logistics firm disrupts the supply of frozen food to thousands of clients, including major franchises like Kentucky Fried Chicken.
ransomware.group: Ransomware Attack Puts a Chill On Japanese Frozen-Food Chain

ransomware.summary: Swiss rail vehicle manufacturer Stadler Rail says the Everest ransomware gang demanded about $12.3 million after breaching a data exchange platform shared with one of its suppliers. [...]
ransomware.group: Swiss rail giant Stadler rejects $12.3M ransom demand after cyberattack

ransomware.summary: Enterprise AI can accelerate ransomware attacks when AI assistants and agents inherit excessive permissions or compromised identities. Acronis explains how identity controls, governance, and least-privilege access help reduce AI-enabled ransomware risk while supporting secure AI adoption. [...]
ransomware.group: How enterprise GenAI can amplify ransomware risk — and how to contain it
